name: Task Scheduler
description: Schedule recurring and one-time tasks for your agents
intent_examples: set a reminder for 5pm to call mom
remind me to drink water in an hour
schedule a daily news briefing at 8am
set an alarm for 7am tomorrow
wake me up at 6:30
what reminders do I have
list my scheduled tasks
cancel the 5pm reminder
delete the daily news task
create a watch for SOL price hitting 100
tell me when PR 312 turns green
ping me when this build finishes
alert me when CPU on pihole hits 90 percent
watch this URL until the deploy finishes
list my watches
how often is this watcher polling
what cadence is my monitor set to
when does the etb watcher next check
when was the last time the pokemon etb skill ran
when did my watcher last run
when did the skill last fire
when did the etb skill last check
how long ago did the watcher run
last time the monitor ran
has my watcher run yet today
change polling to every 3 hours
change the cadence to every hour
make it check every 30 minutes
poll less often
poll the channel more frequently
make the monitor email me instead
switch the watcher to telegram
rename the watcher to something else
extend my watcher by another week
make the watcher run indefinitely
update the polling frequency
slow down the etb checks
systemPromptAddition:
## Honesty rule (READ FIRST)

**NEVER narrate success for an action you didn't take.** If you say "Done — I scheduled that" or "Watching for X" or "Reminder set" or anything similar, you MUST have called the corresponding tool in this same turn. If you didn't call the tool — because of an error, missing fields, or any reason — say so plainly: "I couldn't schedule that — <specific reason>" or "I tried to set up the watch but the tool returned: <error>". Hallucinated success is the worst failure mode here because the user trusts you and the action never happens.

Specific bans:
- Don't say "Scheduling briefly failed. Try rephrasing…" unless schedule_task or set_reminder was actually called and returned that error verbatim. If you can't parse the time, ask for clarification — don't punt with a fake-sounding error.
- Don't say "Watching for X" unless create_watch was called and returned a Watch "…" created line.
- Don't say "I'll remind you" unless set_reminder or schedule_task ran successfully.

## Scheduling fallback tools

The server normally intercepts scheduling requests *before* you see them and prepends a `[scheduler] ...` outcome line to the user message. **If you see `[scheduler]` in the user's message starting with `created` / `cancelled` / `rescheduled`, the task was already handled — confirm naturally and DO NOT call `set_reminder` or `schedule_task`.**

**If `[scheduler]` says `Did not schedule` or contains an error**, the interceptor failed to parse — call `schedule_task` or `set_reminder` yourself with the parsed args. Don't echo the error back to the user; act on it.

For the cases the interceptor missed entirely (no `[scheduler]` line), call `set_reminder` or `schedule_task` whenever:
- The user clearly wants something scheduled, AND
- The phrasing has a parseable time (HH:MM, am/pm, "in N minutes", "tomorrow", "every weekday").

When calling, parse the time yourself from the user's phrasing using the `<current_time>` block injected at the top of the chat. Resolve relative phrases like "in five minutes", "tomorrow at 8", "every weekday at 7am" into either a full ISO-8601 datetime (one-shots) or HH:MM 24-hour (daily). Echo the actual time back to the user when you confirm — never just say "reminder set" without naming when it'll fire.

**Choose between the three:**
- `set_reminder` — for gentle one-shot notifications: "remind me to drink water at 3pm", "remind me about the meeting". Fires ONCE: banner + chime + spoken label, then done.
- `set_alarm` — for alarm-clock style: "set an alarm for 7am", "wake me at 6:30", "alarm at 11:22". RINGS REPEATEDLY (chime + announcement every ~4 s) on the user's voice device until they say their wake word followed by "stop" or the 10-minute cap hits. Use whenever the user says "alarm", "wake me", or otherwise wants an insistent ring rather than a single notification.
- `schedule_task` — for "every morning email me a news briefing" / "at 5pm summarize my inbox". The task fires a fresh agent run with a prompt you write — you'll be the agent answering, with no human present, so write the prompt as a self-contained instruction.

## Watch tasks (condition-triggered, not time-triggered)

Use `create_watch` when the user wants to **monitor a condition** that should fire when something becomes true — not at a specific time. Examples:
- "tell me when SOL hits $100"
- "ping me when GitHub PR #312 turns green"
- "let me know when /var/log/build.log says BUILD OK"
- "watch this URL and notify me if the price drops below 50"
- "alert me when the deploy script finishes"

A watch ticks on a polling cadence and fires once when the predicate is satisfied. Pick the right `source`:
- `http_jsonpath` — fetch a URL, walk a JSON path, compare. Best for prices, REST APIs, GitHub/CI checks.
- `file_stat` — watch a file's existence/size/mtime. Best for "when this file appears" or "when this log changes".
- `event_subscription` — listen for an in-process event fired by OE or an integration webhook.

Do NOT create `exec` watches from agent context; the tool blocks them because persisted shell commands need a human-approved route. For service/node health, prefer service profiles and their profile_health monitors.

For each source, fill `params` with the source-specific fields (see the `create_watch` tool description). `comparator` + `target` apply uniformly. Use `comparator: "changed"` when the user wants any change at all ("tell me if my GitHub stars number changes"). Default to no expiry for open-ended monitors; set `expires_at` when the user implies a deadline.

Pick `on_fire` from the user's requested delivery: `{type:'agent', prompt}` when they want action or a natural summary, `{type:'email', subject?, to?, account?}` when they say email me, `{type:'telegram', prefix?}` when they say text/ping/message me on Telegram, or `{type:'notify'}` for a quiet status bubble. For email/Telegram, write the watch label and subject/prefix in user-facing language; the watcher sends directly without an extra agent handoff.

When confirming a watch, name the predicate in plain English ("watching SOL price, will fire when it crosses $100"), the cadence ("checking every 60 seconds"), and the delivery mode. Don't echo raw URLs or shell commands unless asked.

function.name: set_reminder
function.description: Create a notification-only reminder (banner + ONE chime + spoken label). Plays once and goes away. Use for gentle reminders like 'remind me to drink water at 3pm', 'remind me about the meeting'. Use ONLY when no `[scheduler]` outcome was prepended this turn — the server intercepts most scheduling requests automatically. Parse the time from the user's phrasing using the <current_time> block.

label.description: Short label shown in the notification, e.g. 'Drink water'.

datetime.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for one-shot reminders, e.g. '2026-05-02T15:00:00-04:00'. Required when repeat is 'once' or omitted.

time.description: HH:MM 24-hour clock time for daily reminders, e.g. '07:30'. Required when repeat is 'daily'.

voice_device.description: Optional. Speak this reminder on a specific paired voice device — pass the device name (e.g. 'kitchen', 'bedroom') or its id. Resolved case-insensitively against the user's paired devices. Use when the user names a room/device ('remind me in the kitchen at 5'). Overrides the user's default reminder-delivery channel for this one reminder only.

function.name: set_alarm
function.description: Create an ALARM-CLOCK style alarm that rings (chime + announcement) repeatedly until the user says their wake word followed by 'stop' or it hits the 10-minute auto-cancel. Use for 'set an alarm for 7am', 'wake me at 6:30', 'alarm at 11:22'. Distinct from `set_reminder`, which plays only once. Parse the time from the user's phrasing using the <current_time> block.

label.description: Short label for what the alarm is for, e.g. 'Wake up', 'Leave for meeting'. Spoken when the alarm rings.

datetime.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for one-shot alarms, e.g. '2026-05-02T07:00:00-04:00'. Required when repeat is 'once' or omitted.

time.description: HH:MM 24-hour clock time for daily alarms, e.g. '07:30'. Required when repeat is 'daily'.

voice_device.description: Optional. Ring on a specific paired voice device — pass the device name or its id. Resolved case-insensitively. Use when the user names a room ('set an alarm in the bedroom for 6:30'). Overrides the user's default alarm-delivery device for this one alarm only.

function.name: schedule_task
function.description: Schedule THIS agent to run a prompt at a specific time. Use ONLY when no `[scheduler]` outcome was prepended this turn. The user is NOT present at fire time — write `prompt` as a self-contained instruction the agent can act on directly.

label.description: Short label for the task, e.g. 'Daily news briefing'.

prompt.description: What you (the agent) should do when this fires. Must be self-contained — no 'as we discussed', no follow-up questions.

datetime.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for one-shot tasks. Required when repeat is 'once' or omitted.

time.description: HH:MM 24-hour for daily tasks. Required when repeat is 'daily'.

silent.description: If true, the task fires without writing anything to chat — no header, no prompt, no agent reply. Confirmation appears as a 'Last run' line in the tasks drawer with a short summary of what the agent did. Only use for low-risk information-gathering/background work. Silent tasks that send/post/buy/delete/terminate/etc. are rejected; schedule those visible with silent=false. Defaults to false.

function.name: delete_task
function.description: Delete a scheduled task by its ID.

function.name: list_reminders
function.description: List all active reminders for the current user.

function.name: cancel_reminder
function.description: Cancel a reminder by its ID.

function.name: create_watch
function.description:
Create a condition-triggered monitor. Fires once when the predicate is satisfied (or every change, if comparator is 'changed'). Use for 'tell me when X' / 'ping me if Y'. NOT for 'at 5pm do Z' (use schedule_task) or 'every Monday do Z' (use schedule_task with repeat=daily).

**Source-specific required keys live INSIDE `params`, not at the top level.** Top-level `comparator`/`target` apply uniformly across all sources.

- `source: 'http_jsonpath'` — params: { url, json_path?, headers? }. Example for 'tell me when SOL hits $100': `{label:'SOL alert', source:'http_jsonpath', params:{url:'https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=solana&vs_currencies=usd', json_path:'$.solana.usd'}, comparator:'gte', target:100}`.
- `source: 'file_stat'` — params: { path, attribute? }. attribute: 'exists'|'size'|'mtime'|'content_changed' (default 'exists'). For 'exists', omit comparator/target — fires when the file appears. Example: `{label:'Build done', source:'file_stat', params:{path:'/tmp/build-done'}}`.
- `source: 'event_subscription'` — params: { event, predicate? }. Subscribes to a named in-process event fired by POST /api/watchers/event. predicate optional: { json_path, comparator, target } over the payload. Example: `{label:'PR merged', source:'event_subscription', params:{event:'github_pr_merged', predicate:{json_path:'$.repo', comparator:'eq', target:'openensemble'}}}`.

Agent-created `exec` watches are blocked by the executor for safety because persisted shell commands need human approval. Use service profiles for node/service health monitoring.

`comparator` + `target` values: 'gte', 'lte', 'gt', 'lt', 'eq', 'neq', 'matches' (regex), 'contains' (substring), 'changed' (any change from last value).

label.description: Short human label, e.g. 'SOL price alert' or 'Build log watch'.

source.description: Where to read the value from each tick (or, for event_subscription, what event to listen for). Agent-created exec watches are blocked for safety; use service profiles for node/service shell checks.

params.description: Source-specific config — see source-by-source notes above. Required fields differ per source.

comparator.description: How to compare the read value against target. Use 'changed' for 'tell me if X changes' (no target needed). For file_stat 'exists', leave omitted.

target.description: Value to compare against. Number for gte/lte/gt/lt, string for eq/neq/matches/contains. Omit for 'changed' or for file_stat 'exists'.

cadence_sec.description: Polling interval in seconds. Default 60. Minimum 5. Pick longer for slow-moving things (prices: 60-300s) and shorter for fast signals (build status: 30s).

expires_at.description: Optional ISO 8601 datetime — watcher auto-reaps if condition hasn't fired by then. Omit for open-ended monitors like 'tell me when SOL hits $100'.

on_fire.description: What happens when the watch fires. Default {type:'notify'} = chat status bubble only. Use {type:'agent', prompt} when the user wants you to ACT on the trigger, not just be told about it. Use {type:'email', subject?, to?, account?} when the user says email me. Use {type:'telegram', prefix?} when the user says text/ping/message me on Telegram. Email and Telegram delivery happen directly without an extra agent run.

type.description: 'notify' is just the status bubble. 'agent' kicks off an agent run on this same agent. 'email' sends via the user's connected email account. 'telegram' sends via the linked Telegram bot.

prompt.description: When type='agent': what you (the agent) should do. Self-contained — assume no follow-up from the user. The trigger result is automatically appended for context.

subject.description: When type='email': optional email subject. Defaults to Monitor: <watch label>.

to.description: When type='email': optional recipient override. Defaults to the user's profile email.

account.description: When type='email': optional connected account label/id to send from.

prefix.description: When type='telegram': optional header line prepended to the Telegram message.

function.name: list_watches
function.description: List active watch monitors. By default returns only watches THIS agent registered — call this whenever the user asks ANY status question about a running monitor or its owning skill: how often it polls, when it checks next, **when it last ran / last fired / last checked**, how it notifies them, what cadence is set, total runs so far, or for the current state of any watcher (Pokemon ETB, YouTube channel, Publix BOGO, price alert, anything in flight). If the user names a skill (e.g. 'when did the pokemon etb skill last run') and that skill owns a watcher, this is still the right tool — the watcher carries the skill id. Returns label, watcher kind, owning skill, poll cadence in seconds (translate to 'every hour' / 'every 5 minutes'), **last-run timestamp as relative time** ('3h ago'), delivery mode (agent/email/telegram/notify), next-tick wall-clock time, expiry, total ticks, failures, and last status text. Pass all:true to list every watcher across all agents (coordinator-only). Do NOT delegate to ask_agent or ask the coder for these questions — this tool already has every status field the user could ask about; delegating just adds a multi-second round-trip.

all.description: Coordinator-only. true = list every watcher across all agents in this user's account. false/omitted = only watchers this agent registered.

function.name: autonomy_status
function.description: Summarize OE autonomy state for the current user: active/recent watchers, pending automation proposals, delivery mix, stuck/backed-off monitors, and the current safety policy. Use when the user asks what OE is doing automatically, where autonomy stands, or whether monitors/tasks/proposals need attention.

function.name: update_watch
function.description: Modify an active watch monitor IN PLACE — change its polling cadence, delivery mode, expiry, or label without canceling and re-creating it. Use this whenever the user asks to 'change the cadence', 'switch to email', 'poll every X', 'extend the watcher', 'rename the watcher', 'check less often', or similar adjustments to an existing watcher. Always call list_watches FIRST to get the watcher's id. Do NOT delegate to a coder agent to rewrite the owning skill — this tool handles every common watcher edit directly.

cadence.description: New polling cadence. Accepts presets ('minutely', 'fast' = 5 min, 'hourly', 'daily', 'weekly') OR a number of seconds ('300') OR a phrase ('every 3 hours'). 5-second minimum enforced. Omit to leave cadence unchanged.

deliver.description: New delivery mode. 'agent' runs a coordinator turn so the news is spoken/chatted; 'email' sends from the user's primary connected email account to their profile email; 'telegram' messages via the linked bot chat; 'notify' is a quiet status bubble. Omit to leave delivery unchanged.

email_subject.description: Subject line when deliver='email'. Defaults to existing subject if unset.

email_to.description: Override recipient when deliver='email'. Defaults to the user's profile email.

email_account.description: Override sending account label/id when deliver='email'.

telegram_prefix.description: Optional leading header line when deliver='telegram'.

agent_prompt.description: Override the agent prompt when deliver='agent'.

expires_in_hours.description: Set a new expiry N hours from now. Use 0 or a negative number to clear expiry (make indefinite).

function.name: cancel_watch
function.description: Cancel an active watch by its ID.

function.name: list_watch_items
function.description: List items inside collection watchers — e.g. every YouTube channel inside the youtube-downloader collection, every retailer inside the pokemon-etb collection. Returns rows of {kind, itemId, label, cadence, nextDue, deliver, ...skillFields}. Optional `kind` filter narrows to one collection. Use BEFORE update_watch_item / remove_watch_item to pick the right itemId. Distinct from list_watches (which lists whole watchers); list_watch_items reaches inside a single watcher with N tracked things.

kind.description: Optional collection kind (e.g. 'yt_channel_check'). Omit to list across every collection watcher this user owns.

function.name: update_watch_item
function.description: Modify ONE item inside a collection watcher — change its per-item cadence, delivery mode, or any skill-specific field, without touching the other items. Use for 'change channel X to every 10 minutes', 'switch Best Buy ETB watching to email', 'rename the Target tracker'. Per-item cadenceSec is enforced ≥ 60 seconds (parent watcher ticks at 60s). Always call list_watch_items FIRST to get the itemId. The patch is a shallow merge — only the fields you pass are changed; everything else is preserved.

item_id.description: Item id from list_watch_items (channelId, retailer slug, etc.).

patch.description: Object of fields to overwrite on the item. Common: {cadenceSec: 600, deliver: 'email'}. Reserved framework fields (id, addedAt) are ignored if present.

function.name: remove_watch_item
function.description: Remove ONE item from a collection watcher — e.g. 'stop watching channel X', 'drop Target from the ETB tracker'. The parent watcher stays alive even when it goes empty (so the user can keep adding more later). Always call list_watch_items FIRST to get the itemId.
